specs.title: Panier client offline (#33): route the storefront cart through the durable IndexedDB outbox
tables: none - no schema change. Reuses existing orders.client_request_id UNIQUE idempotency (migration 20260628010000) already in prod.
specs.migrationSqlDraft:
-- No migration for this chantier. Client-side outbox wiring only.
-- The server safety net it relies on already exists and is additive-in-history:
--   * migration 20260628010000 added orders.client_request_id + UNIQUE index +
--     create_order_with_items dedup, and REVOKE PUBLIC/anon + GRANT service_role.
--   * /api/orders already short-circuits on findOrderByClientRequestId BEFORE any
--     side effect (coupon claim, insert, destock), so an outbox replay of the same
--     client_request_id returns {deduplicated:true} and destocks exactly once.
-- Nothing to add. If a future audit wants replay observability, that is a separate
-- chantier (additive orders.replayed_at column), explicitly OUT OF SCOPE here.
serviceChanges: none - order.service.ts is PROTECTED and already exposes findOrderByClientRequestId + idempotent createOrderWithItems used by /api/orders. No service method added or modified.

Reuse (no change): src/lib/offline/submit-order.ts submitOrder() and replayOrderEntry(); src/lib/offline/outbox.ts createOutbox(); src/lib/offline/outbox-idb.ts getOrderOutbox() singleton (shared DB 'attabl-offline'/store 'order-outbox').
Reuse (no change): src/hooks/useOrderOutbox.ts (drain on mount/online/visibilitychange/interval) and src/hooks/useNetworkStatus.ts.
uiComponents: EDIT src/app/sites/[site]/(storefront)/cart/page.tsx handleSubmitOrder(): replace the raw fetch('/api/orders') block with submitOrder({ endpoint:'/api/orders', body:{ tableNumber, items:orderItems, notes, display_currency:displayCurrency, tip_amount, coupon_code:appliedCoupon?.code }, clientRequestId: clientRequestIdRef.current }). Remove client_request_id from body (submitOrder injects it). Branch on result.status: 'sent' -> existing path (persist orderId, clearCart, router.push order-confirmed?orderId=...); 'queued' -> retire clientRequestIdRef (order now owned by outbox), clearCart, reset tip, router.push(`/sites/${slug}/order-confirmed?queued=1`); 'rejected' -> setError(result.error) + setPreviewErrors(result.details ?? []), keep cart + keep clientRequestIdRef (retriable); 'failed' -> setError(t('connectionError')).

EDIT same file: guard the preview call for offline. Wrap the pre-submit fetch('/api/orders/preview') so that when navigator.onLine === false OR the preview fetch throws (transient network), skip validation and fall through to submitOrder (which durably queues). Preview must never block the offline path; server re-validates on replay.

NEW src/components/tenant/client/StorefrontOfflineIndicator.tsx ('use client'): convive-themed analog of admin OfflineIndicator. Calls useNetworkStatus() + useOrderOutbox(). Renders nothing when isOnline && !wasOffline && pending===0 && rejected.length===0. Offline row: WifiOff + t('tenant.offlineNotice') on bg-[var(--color-ink)] text-white. Restored row: RefreshCw (animate-spin) + common.connectionRestored on bg-[var(--color-brand)] text-white. Pending row: UploadCloud + t('tenant.orderQueued',{count}) on bg-[var(--color-brand-light)] text-[var(--color-brand-dark)]. Rejected row: AlertTriangle + t('tenant.orderQueuedFailed',{count}) on bg-[var(--color-promo)]/10 text-[var(--color-promo)], dismissible via shadcn <Button variant=ghost> calling dismissRejected. Non-fixed: rendered as first child of the flex column so it pushes <main> (do NOT use fixed/h-screen/h-dvh - keep the viewport chain intact).

EDIT src/app/sites/[site]/(storefront)/layout.tsx: mount <StorefrontOfflineIndicator /> as the FIRST child of the div.tenant-client flex column (before <main id=main-content>), so the outbox drains anywhere in the convive app (menu, cart, order list) and the customer sees pending/rejected state everywhere.

EDIT src/app/sites/[site]/(storefront)/order-confirmed/page.tsx: add an early branch in OrderConfirmedContent - when !orderId && searchParams.get('queued')==='1', render a QueuedConfirmation view reusing the existing confirmation shell (check-pop circle) but with t('tenant.orderQueuedTitle') + t('tenant.orderQueuedSub'), no order number, no realtime/supabase fetch, and CTAs 'Voir le menu' (Link menuPath) + 'Retour a l'accueil'. This prevents the offline path from hitting the orderNotFound screen. Skip the supabase fetch and realtime effects in queued mode (early return before those effects run / effects already no-op when !orderId).
shadcn primitives used: Button (dismiss on indicator; CTAs on queued confirmation). Icons: lucide WifiOff/RefreshCw/UploadCloud/AlertTriangle/Check. No native elements.
testDbCases: tests/integration/stock/cart-offline-idempotency.test.ts (pnpm test:db, real local Supabase seeded via tests/journeys/fixtures/seed.ts): seed a Pro tenant (canAccessInventory=true) with 1 menu_item backed by 1 ingredient recipe and known opening stock. POST /api/orders with client_request_id=X for that item; assert 200 + orderId returned. POST /api/orders AGAIN with the SAME client_request_id=X (simulates outbox replay): assert response.deduplicated===true and same orderId/orderNumber.

Same test, stock assertion: after the two identical POSTs, assert exactly ONE order row exists for X and the ingredient was destocked exactly once (get_stock_status / stock_movements shows a single order_destock movement for that order, not double). Proves the offline replay path cannot oversell.

Idempotency-before-side-effect: seed a single-use coupon; POST twice with same client_request_id + coupon_code; assert coupon usage counter incremented once (the dedup short-circuit + unclaimUsage-on-dedup path holds), confirming replay does not double-claim.

offline path: mock navigator.onLine=false; submit -> preview fetch NOT called, submitOrder returns {status:'queued'} -> router.push to order-confirmed?queued=1, clearCart called, and the next submit mints a fresh clientRequestId (ref retired).
rejected: submitOrder returns {status:'rejected',error,details} -> setError(error) shown, previewErrors===details, clearCart NOT called, same clientRequestId reused on retry (dedupe key preserved).
failed: submitOrder returns {status:'failed'} -> connectionError shown, cart preserved.
transient preview blip while online: preview fetch rejects -> handler falls through to submitOrder (queued), no thrown error surfaced to user.

StorefrontOfflineIndicator (mock useNetworkStatus + useOrderOutbox): returns null when online/no-pending/no-rejected; renders offline banner when isOnline=false; renders pending banner with count when pending>0; renders rejected banner + dismiss button calling dismissRejected when rejected.length>0.
order-confirmed queued branch: render with searchParams queued=1 and no orderId -> QueuedConfirmation shown (orderQueuedTitle), createClient()/rpc NOT called, orderNotFound NOT shown.

risks: clientRequestIdRef MUST be retired on the 'queued' branch (not only 'sent'). If not, a subsequent DIFFERENT cart reusing the same key would dedupe against the queued order and silently drop the new order.

Offline preview-skip means server-side validation (price/availability) only runs on replay drain. A stale/removed item queued offline is permanently rejected on drain and surfaces via the rejected banner AFTER the customer left the cart. Acceptable (order was never valid) but document; do not silently swallow.

order-confirmed queued branch must early-return before the supabase fetch + realtime useEffect so no network call fires with a null orderId; verify the existing effects already no-op when !orderId and add the explicit queued view branch cleanly.

Viewport: the indicator is an in-flow flex child (NOT fixed, NOT h-dvh/h-screen) so it must not break the (storefront) layout chain html/body overflow:hidden -> .tenant-client h-dvh -> main flex-1 overflow-y-auto. Adding an auto-height banner above <main> is safe; a fixed banner would overlap sticky page headers.

Theme: storefront forces light theme with --color-* convive tokens; the new indicator must use those tokens, NOT the admin dark tokens (bg-app-bg/text-app-text/accent) used by src/components/admin/OfflineIndicator.tsx. Do not reuse the admin component.

Shared IndexedDB outbox singleton: on a device used for BOTH convive ordering and staff POS (rare), pending entries for /api/orders and /api/orders/pos coexist; they stay isolated by entry.endpoint + client_request_id, but the pending count shown to a customer could include staff orders. Low likelihood on customer phones; note.
i18n merge conflicts in fr-FR/en-US.json are near-certain across the stock program - land keys in a small isolated block and sequence the JSON edits, or expect a trivial rebase.
submitLock.current + isSubmitting must be reset in finally for the queued path too, so the CTA re-enables and no double-submit occurs before navigation.

specs.title: Anti-vol: author (created_by) in stock history + 'sorties par employe' report + audible bip on low-stock realtime alert
tables: stock_movements: NO schema change (created_by uuid + supplier_id uuid already exist from 20260212000001 / 20260215000001; created_by has no FK). This chantier ONLY adds two read RPCs on top of the existing table.
admin_users: read-only, joined on (user_id = stock_movements.created_by AND tenant_id = stock_movements.tenant_id) to resolve full_name. No change.
OPTIONAL additive index (perf for report): CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stock_movements_created_by ON public.stock_movements(tenant_id, created_by) WHERE created_by IS NOT NULL;
specs.migrationSqlDraft:
-- Migration: 20260701120000_stock_author_and_staff_report.sql
-- ADDITIVE ONLY: two SECURITY DEFINER read RPCs + one partial index. No drops.
-- Rollback: DROP FUNCTION get_stock_movements_page(uuid,int,int),
--           get_staff_stock_report(uuid,timestamptz,timestamptz); DROP INDEX idx_stock_movements_created_by;
-- NOTE: author_name is only populated for movements whose created_by is set.
--   Manual movements already stamp created_by (adjust_ingredient_stock_tx).
--   order_destock / order_restock / opening only carry an author AFTER the
--   'ledger' chantier stamps created_by on those RPCs -> hard dependency.

-- 0. Perf index for the per-author report -----------------------------------
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stock_movements_created_by
  ON public.stock_movements(tenant_id, created_by)
  WHERE created_by IS NOT NULL;

-- 1. Enriched, paginated movement feed (adds author_name + flattens joins) ---
-- Replaces the client-side PostgREST select (which cannot embed admin_users:
-- created_by has no FK, and user_id is not unique across tenants). Scalar
-- subqueries avoid any join fan-out. Tenant-scoped by assert_tenant_member.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_stock_movements_page(
  p_tenant_id uuid,
  p_limit int DEFAULT 200,
  p_offset int DEFAULT 0
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  id uuid,
  tenant_id uuid,
  ingredient_id uuid,
  movement_type text,
  quantity numeric,
  reference_id uuid,
  notes text,
  created_by uuid,
  supplier_id uuid,
  created_at timestamptz,
  ingredient_name text,
  ingredient_unit text,
  supplier_name text,
  author_name text
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $$
BEGIN
  PERFORM public.assert_tenant_member(p_tenant_id);
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    sm.id, sm.tenant_id, sm.ingredient_id, sm.movement_type, sm.quantity,
    sm.reference_id, sm.notes, sm.created_by, sm.supplier_id, sm.created_at,
    (SELECT i.name FROM public.ingredients i WHERE i.id = sm.ingredient_id) AS ingredient_name,
    (SELECT i.unit FROM public.ingredients i WHERE i.id = sm.ingredient_id) AS ingredient_unit,
    (SELECT s.name FROM public.suppliers s WHERE s.id = sm.supplier_id) AS supplier_name,
    (SELECT au.full_name FROM public.admin_users au
       WHERE au.user_id = sm.created_by AND au.tenant_id = sm.tenant_id
       LIMIT 1) AS author_name
  FROM public.stock_movements sm
  WHERE sm.tenant_id = p_tenant_id
  ORDER BY sm.created_at DESC
  LIMIT GREATEST(COALESCE(p_limit, 200), 0)
  OFFSET GREATEST(COALESCE(p_offset, 0), 0);
END;
$$;
REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION public.get_stock_movements_page(uuid, int, int) FROM PUBLIC, anon;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.get_stock_movements_page(uuid, int, int) TO authenticated, service_role;

-- 2. 'Sorties par employe' aggregate report ----------------------------------
-- Groups movements by author over a date window. out_qty = total withdrawn
-- (abs of negative quantities), in_qty = total added, plus a per-type breakdown
-- so the anti-theft view can isolate manual_remove / negative adjustments.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_staff_stock_report(
  p_tenant_id uuid,
  p_start timestamptz,
  p_end timestamptz
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  author_id uuid,
  author_name text,
  out_qty numeric,
  in_qty numeric,
  movements_count bigint,
  manual_remove_qty numeric,
  adjustment_out_qty numeric,
  order_destock_qty numeric
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $$
BEGIN
  PERFORM public.assert_tenant_member(p_tenant_id);
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    sm.created_by AS author_id,
    (SELECT au.full_name FROM public.admin_users au
       WHERE au.user_id = sm.created_by AND au.tenant_id = sm.tenant_id
       LIMIT 1) AS author_name,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN sm.quantity < 0 THEN -sm.quantity ELSE 0 END), 0) AS out_qty,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN sm.quantity > 0 THEN  sm.quantity ELSE 0 END), 0) AS in_qty,
    COUNT(*) AS movements_count,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN sm.movement_type = 'manual_remove' THEN -sm.quantity ELSE 0 END), 0) AS manual_remove_qty,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN sm.movement_type = 'adjustment' AND sm.quantity < 0 THEN -sm.quantity ELSE 0 END), 0) AS adjustment_out_qty,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN sm.movement_type = 'order_destock' THEN -sm.quantity ELSE 0 END), 0) AS order_destock_qty
  FROM public.stock_movements sm
  WHERE sm.tenant_id = p_tenant_id
    AND sm.created_by IS NOT NULL
    AND sm.created_at >= p_start
    AND sm.created_at <  p_end
  GROUP BY sm.created_by
  ORDER BY out_qty DESC;
END;
$$;
REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION public.get_staff_stock_report(uuid, timestamptz, timestamptz) FROM PUBLIC, anon;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.get_staff_stock_report(uuid, timestamptz, timestamptz) TO authenticated, service_role;

specs.migrationSqlDraft:
-- supabase/migrations/20260701120000_ingredient_purchase_unit_conversion.sql
-- Chantier #15 Conversion d'unites. ADDITIVE ONLY: 2 new columns on the existing
-- RLS-protected ingredients table. No new RPC on purpose - the purchase->base
-- conversion is a pure TS helper, so stock_movements keeps its "always in base
-- unit" invariant and adjust_ingredient_stock_tx's reconcilable ledger is untouched.
-- RLS/index: none needed (columns live on an already-policied table; no lookups
-- by these columns). idx_ingredients_tenant already covers tenant scoping.
--
-- Rollback: ALTER TABLE public.ingredients
--   DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS ingredients_units_per_purchase_positive,
--   DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS units_per_purchase, DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS purchase_unit;

ALTER TABLE public.ingredients
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS purchase_unit TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS units_per_purchase NUMERIC(10,3) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1;

ALTER TABLE public.ingredients
  DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS ingredients_units_per_purchase_positive;
ALTER TABLE public.ingredients
  ADD CONSTRAINT ingredients_units_per_purchase_positive
  CHECK (units_per_purchase > 0);

COMMENT ON COLUMN public.ingredients.purchase_unit IS
  'Optional purchasing-unit label (casier, sac, carton). NULL = purchased in base unit.';
COMMENT ON COLUMN public.ingredients.units_per_purchase IS
  'Base units (ingredients.unit) contained in one purchase_unit. Default 1.';
serviceChanges: src/lib/inventory/unit-conversion.ts (NEW): export convertToBaseUnit({ quantity, baseUnit, purchaseUnit, unitsPerPurchase }): number and isSiConvertible(a,b): boolean. Logic: if purchaseUnit is null/equal to baseUnit -> quantity; else if both baseUnit and purchaseUnit are SI-convertible (g/kg, cl/L only in current enum) -> use convert-units lib convert(qty).from(purchaseUnit).to(baseUnit); else custom unit (casier/sac/bouteille/piece) -> quantity * unitsPerPurchase. COLLISION MODELE (majeure): recipes.ingredient_id + destock_order/restock_order (20260212000001 + 20260629120000, ce dernier PROTEGE via order.service cote route) sont tenant-wide et NE connaissent PAS venue_id. Mettre venue_id sur ingredients cree des lignes ingredient par venue -> quelle ligne destocke une commande? destock_order tomberait sur des lignes venue_id NULL ou en dupliquerait. Il faut soit (a) resoudre le venue depuis orders.venue_id dans une NOUVELLE RPC destock (jamais toucher order.service.ts), soit (b) separer definition tenant-wide et stock par venue dans une table dediee ingredient_venue_stock. Le sketch choisit venue_id nullable (NULL=principal) pour rester additif SANS casser le destock existant, mais le vrai multi-venue exige la decision (b) - a trancher AVANT dev.

BACKFILL: choix NULL=venue principal evite un backfill destructif. Mais si un tenant a deja 2 venues, son stock actuel reste 'global' (NULL) jusqu'a migration explicite opt-in. Documenter que NULL != un venue nomme.
